scope.summary: Commits the C7 fixture: a green test pinning that a page with rows reports its select-list columns, and a committed-red test asserting a zero-row page must do the same, which A1c flips by populating QueryPageBuilder from statement describe metadata.
out_of_scope: Populating columns from describe metadata — A1c owns the fix; this bead owns only the fixture.
Changing QueryPageBuilder::new's signature, which the fix requires and this bead deliberately does not make.
The oracle_describe not-found contract (A1d) and the live e2e ladder that asserts it; tracked separately.

entry_points.name: oracle_query page construction (QueryPageBuilder -> QueryResponse.columns)
entry_points.trace: c7_a_page_with_rows_reports_its_columns builds a response through query_response_from_rows_checked and asserts the select-list columns are reported, pinning the working half of the contract that the zero-row case violates

limitations: Scoped test only: cargo test -p oraclemcp-db --lib (452 passed, 1 expected-failure) plus the deliberate red run of that expected failure. The full workspace gate was not run for this commit.
The zero-row assertion is committed red by design and does not execute in the default run; it is not cited as proof of anything here.

The flip is NOT attribute-only, unlike C1-C3. QueryPageBuilder::new has no metadata parameter today, so a fixture that compiles now cannot call the post-fix constructor. A1c must change the construction line as well as delete the #[ignore]; the assertion is the invariant and must stay byte-identical.

The fixture asserts at the QueryPageBuilder seam, not through a live oracle_query against a VPD-filtered table. It proves the page forgets its schema; it does not by itself prove the end-to-end agent-visible symptom on a real database.
follow_ups: A1c must delete the #[ignore] on query::tests::c7_a_zero_row_page_still_reports_its_columns and pass statement describe metadata into QueryPageBuilder::new, leaving the assertion unchanged.
